title: viola philippica
intro: Viola philippica, also known as the butterfly orchid viola, is a species of flowering plant in the viola genus, characterized by its distinctive purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Viola philippica are attributed to its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Viola philippica can be found in the form of infusions, salves, and creams, which are often used topically to take advantage of the plant's medicinal properties.
